title: Humboltia diptera
taxonomy: Humboldtia diptera is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to Borneo and is also known as the 'flytrap tree' due to its unique seed pods resembling a flytrap. The classification of Humboldtia diptera includes its kingdom as Plantae, its phylum as Magnoliophyta, and its class as Rosopsida. Further classification includes its order as Sapindales, its family as Sapindaceae, its genus as Humboldtia, and its species as H. diptera. This classification reflects its relationships within the plant kingdom and its distinct characteristics.

intro: Humboldtia diptera, also known as the "Indian tobacco plant," is a flowering plant native to the tropical forests of India.
